Name: MSU Space Cowboys
Relevance: Nationally competitive high-powered rocketry team; designs and launches sounding rockets at competitions like the Spaceport America Cup.

Name: CubeSat at MSU
Relevance: Student-led team developing Mississippi's first satellite (1-Mississippi) and collaborating on the CAPE-4 mission payload.

Name: Robotics Team at Mississippi State (MSSR)
Relevance: Competes in NASA-based competitions including Lunabotics and the Lunar Life Odyssey; focuses on space-related robotics.

Name: Xipiter MSU
Relevance: Autonomous UAS competition team focused on guidance, navigation, and control; often serves as a payload testing platform for space projects.

Name: August Raspet Student Branch (AIAA)
Relevance: Official student chapter of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics; central hub for aerospace networking and technical activities.

Name: Raspet Flight Research Laboratory
Relevance: Historic flight research facility specializing in UAS development, composite aircraft structures, and experimental aviation.

Name: Geosystems Research Institute (GRI)
Relevance: Focuses on satellite data analysis and remote sensing toolkit development, including planetary data integration.

Name: Advanced Composites Institute (ACI)
Relevance: Develops advanced stitched composite manufacturing technologies originally created at NASA for high-performance aerospace applications.

Name: Plasma and Combustion Diagnostics Lab
Relevance: Conducts laser diagnostics in plasma and combustion environments, essential for advancing aerospace propulsion research.

Name: Physics & Astronomy Research - Astronomy Group
Relevance: Academic research in extrasolar planets, host star characterization, and comet science using mission-based satellite data.

Name: MSU Astronomy Club
Relevance: Amateur astronomy group focused on telescopic viewing, celestial observation, and public science outreach.

Name: Sigma Gamma Tau
Relevance: National honor society for aerospace engineering recognizing outstanding student achievement in the field.

Name: ASSURE (FAA Center of Excellence for UAS)
Relevance: MSU-led coalition of universities conducting research to safely integrate UAS into the national airspace system.

Name: Atmospheric Electroaerodynamic Propulsion Team
Relevance: Student team that developed and validated an ion propulsion system for the 2024 AIAA Region II Student Conference.

Name: Center for Advanced Vehicular Systems (CAVS)
Relevance: Researches autonomous navigation and advanced materials; highly relevant to the development of planetary rovers and robotics.

Name: Northern Gulf Institute (NGI)
Relevance: Utilizes satellite remote sensing and UAS platforms for high-resolution environmental and oceanic data collection.

Name: Fatigue and Fracture Testing Laboratory
Relevance: Specializes in testing advanced aerospace materials and structures under extreme environmental and loading conditions.

Name: Bulldog Motorsports (Formula SAE)
Relevance: Focuses on aerodynamics, internal combustion, and lightweight structures for formula-style racing cars, sharing engineering principles with aerospace.

Name: Mud Dawg Motorsports (Baja SAE)
Relevance: Designs off-road vehicles requiring rugged mechanical design similar to requirements for planetary exploration mobility systems.

Name: High Performance Computing Collaboratory (HPC2)
Relevance: Provides computational simulation resources for fluid dynamics and structural modeling critical to aerospace and propulsion design.

Name: Mississippi Space Grant Consortium (MSSGC)
Relevance: Statewide program funded by NASA that supports aerospace scholarships, internships, and student-led space research at MSU.
